editorial_angle: The design-to-code handoff has been the leaky pipe in software development for decades. Fidelity dies in translation. Google just patched it with a portable format and free tools that speak directly to the coding agents builders are already using. Apple, the same week, used its platform control to freeze the vibe coding apps that were closing this gap on mobile. This isn't a coincidence. It's a fork in the road for AI-native creation: one platform opening the pipeline, the other welding it shut. Tayler's 20 years as a designer before becoming an engineer gives him standing to say what most commentators can't: this handoff problem is real, DESIGN.md is the most promising attempt at solving it he's seen, and Apple's move tells you exactly how threatened the status quo is.
key_message: Apple froze the tools closing the design-to-code gap on mobile the same week Google made those tools free on desktop — and the timing isn't an accident.

primary.name: MacRumors — Apple Blocks Updates for Vibe Coding Apps

supporting.name: Google AI Blog — Stitch AI UI Design

visual_metaphor_direction.core_tension: Two gates side by side: Apple's is slammed shut with a padlock and a 'Blocked' sign on the Replit logo. Google's gate is wide open with a free-entry sign and a clear path through. A builder figure stands between them holding a design file, forced to choose a side.
key_elements: Apple logo on a locked gate or wall
Google logo on an open gate or door
DESIGN.md as a document or blueprint passing freely through the Google side
A single builder/developer figure caught between the two
avoid: Generic tech imagery with no named brands
Happy or optimistic framing for both sides — this is a conflict, not a partnership
Literal App Store screenshots or UI mockups
Both companies depicted as equal — Apple is the antagonist this week

x.hook: Apple blocked Replit. Google shipped free design-to-code tools. Same week.
x.angle: The timing is too clean to be coincidental — Apple knows what's at stake if AI-native creation tools mature outside their ecosystem, and blocking updates is the only lever they have left

linkedin.hook: I spent 20 years losing fidelity in the design-to-code handoff. DESIGN.md might actually fix it — if Apple lets the tools that use it survive.
linkedin.angle: Frame it as a practical question for engineering and product leaders: what does your design-to-code workflow cost you today, and how exposed is it to platform gatekeeping decisions you can't control?

bluesky.hook: Apple froze Replit updates this week. Google shipped DESIGN.md for free. I keep looking at the timing.
bluesky.angle: Conversational, builder-to-builder: who's tried Stitch yet? Does the HTML export actually hold up in production? This is a genuine question Tayler wants answered.

do_not_include: Paul Graham's vague Replit tease — too speculative without a product name
Claude Code v2.1.79 release notes — incremental update
JetBrains Koog Java framework — niche enterprise
NVIDIA AI-Q blueprint — enterprise infrastructure story
Any mention of the post being AI-generated, automated, or pipeline-produced
References to 'agents', 'pipelines', or how the content was found
